module_map.responsibility: Base Expression class hierarchy; defines Expr, Func, Binary, Unary metaclasses and tree traversal API
String manipulation functions: Soundex, Length, Replace, Levenshtein, etc.
Converts token stream into AST; defines parsing rules, operator precedence, and dialect-specific parse functions
Token and TokenType definitions; interfaces with Tokenizer for lexical analysis
Core tokenization logic (compiled with mypyc); converts SQL string into token stream
AST to SQL conversion; dialect-specific SQL generation from expression tree
Dialect registry and factory; manages tokenizer, parser, and generator per dialect
Query optimization passes: predicate pushdown, subquery elimination, join elimination, etc.
SQL execution engine; evaluates AST directly (not a compiler, but an interpreter)

types_produced: Expr subtypes: Select, Insert, Update, Delete, Create, Alter, etc.

pipeline_trace.key_function: Expr.__init__, tree assembly via nested Expression nodes
types_produced: Complete AST with type-specific Expression nodes (Func, Column, Literal, etc.)

pipeline_trace.key_function: Optimizer passes modify AST in-place or return new tree

types_produced: Final SQL string in target dialect

cross_module_hubs.reason: Entry point for all parsing; imports from tokenizer_core, expressions, dialects, errors; called by Dialect.parse()
Base class for all expression types; imported by parser, generator, optimizer, all expression submodules
Converts AST to SQL; depends on all expression types; overridden per-dialect; called by Dialect.generate()
Registry and factory for dialects; imports tokenizer, parser, generator per dialect; coordinates pipeline
Central export hub; re-exports all expression submodules; generates FUNCTION_BY_NAME registry

change_proposal.description: Add LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2) function support across dialects (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, BigQuery, etc.)
new_types: Levenshtein (already exists in expressions/string.py with args: this, expression, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: SOUNDEX and EDITDISTANCE patterns in expressions/string.py; Soundex is minimal (class Soundex(Expression, Func): pass) while Levenshtein has detailed arg_types
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Parser: Add LEVENSHTEIN keyword mapping in Parser.FUNCTIONS dict (dialect-specific). 2. Generator: Add dialect-specific sql_levenshtein() override (if syntax differs). 3. Dialects: Map function via parser_class.FUNCTIONS[LEVENSHTEIN] = build_levenshtein
steps: 1. Verify Levenshtein expression class in expressions/string.py (already present: lines 74-82)
2. Add parser support: Map 'LEVENSHTEIN' keyword to exp.Levenshtein in dialect parsers
3. Add generator support: Implement levenshtein_sql() method in dialect generators (or inherit default)
4. Test transpilation: Parse LEVENSHTEIN from source dialect, generate to target dialect
5. Handle cost arguments: Some dialects only support (str1, str2); others support (str1, str2, max_dist)
risks: Signature variance: PostgreSQL supports (str1, str2) returning integer; MySQL supports LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2); Snowflake uses EDITDISTANCE; BigQuery has no native LEVENSHTEIN (requires UDF)
Cost arguments: ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost are not universally supported; must handle gracefully in transpilation
NULL handling: Behavior differs by dialect (PostgreSQL returns NULL if either arg is NULL; MySQL returns NULL)
Performance: Some dialects implement Levenshtein-Damerau distance (true edit distance) while others implement simple Levenshtein
Missing dialect support: BigQuery, DuckDB may require workarounds or UDF implementations

tool_usage.params: Located top-level Python modules and subdirectories in sqlglot/
Discovered structure of sqlglot/sqlglot/ directory and located key files

Examined __init__.py (public API and transpile pipeline), tokens.py (token types), parser.py (parsing rules), expressions/string.py (Soundex, Levenshtein), expressions/__init__.py (expression export hub)
Searched for SOUNDEX and EDITDISTANCE keywords to locate pattern examples; searched for cross-module imports to identify dependency hubs
